response.justification: Bogdan Szabo has extensive software development experience, particularly in full-stack and tooling roles, but his background lacks direct alignment with the embedded networking and security-focused requirements of this role. While his C/C++ experience at National Instruments and low-latency work at Sociomantic are relevant, his recent work has been heavily TypeScript/Node.js-focused, and there is no clear evidence of embedded firmware or LAN-switch product development. His Japanese language proficiency is also absent, which is a hard requirement.
strengths: Strong C/C++ experience from early career (National Instruments, Sociomantic), including low-latency systems.
Diverse technical background with exposure to networking (HTTP, load balancing) and open-source contributions.
Proven ability to learn and adapt (e.g., taking over iOS development, functional programming workshops).
concerns: No demonstrated experience with embedded firmware or LAN-switch products, which are core to the role.
Lacks native-level Japanese proficiency, a mandatory requirement.
Recent work is primarily in TypeScript/Node.js, with no clear evidence of Linux kernel modification or security-focused development.
